# FUGU

> FUGU is a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) platform that classifies each
> incoming query and automatically routes it to a vector search backend
> (pgvector), a graph traversal backend (Apache AGE), or both — then grounds
> the generated answer in the retrieved chunks.

FUGU is aimed at teams who want production-grade RAG infrastructure without
choosing upfront between vector search and graph search, and who need strict
per-organization data isolation.

## Key facts

- Query routing: an embedding-centroid classifier decides per-query whether
  to use vector search, graph search, or a hybrid of both — no manual backend
  selection required.
- Tenant isolation: every document, chunk, and graph path is scoped to
  organization_id at the database (row-level security) layer, not just
  filtered in application code.
- Bring your own key (BYOK): answer generation runs on the customer's own
  LLM API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, Grok); FUGU never
  charges markup on model usage.
- Document ingestion: supports PDF, DOCX, CSV, XLSX, JSON, and plain text,
  parsed and chunked automatically.
- Compliance: GDPR and Turkish KVKK aligned, with self-serve data export and
  a documented erasure workflow.
- Security: SSRF-hardened outbound webhooks, complete audit logging of
  mutating actions, API key management, team roles.

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://fugu-routes.com/): product overview, pricing, security posture.
- [Docs](https://fugu-routes.com/docs): API reference, SDK quick start.
- [FAQ](https://fugu-routes.com/faq): common questions about routing, BYOK, pricing, security.
- [FUGU vs Pinecone](https://fugu-routes.com/compare/pinecone): comparison for teams evaluating a managed vector database vs. a routed RAG platform.
- [FUGU vs LangChain](https://fugu-routes.com/compare/langchain): comparison for teams evaluating a DIY orchestration framework vs. a managed RAG platform.

## Contact

For questions not covered by the docs or FAQ, sign up at https://fugu-routes.com/sign-up
and use the in-dashboard support page.
